decisions: FastAPI backend with vanilla JS frontend
All endpoints GET-only for public safety
Dark theme with auto-refresh every 5 seconds
Task decomposition in Kloutbot, not broker
Orchestration loop polls for subtask completion
Priority levels: critical, high, normal, low
Task-type routing with ordered agent lists
Thread-safe with RLocks for agents, intents, stats
Flask REST API wrapper for broker
Supports both HTTP and file-based agent communication
Markdown-based task memory with standard template
Context packs under 10KB for session bootstrap
Event-driven hooks for automatic memory updates
System learning now aggregates trades, mesh, orchestration, security, and registry churn.

limitations: Depends on other agents for implementation
kloutbot.notes: Primary orchestration agent. Decomposes complex tasks into subtasks and dispatches to appropriate builders.

claude_cowork.role: Primary code builder and implementation agent
claude_cowork.notes: Primary builder in the pool. Handles code_task, code_editing, planning, scaffolding, test_harness, spec, architecture, docs.

perplexity_research.notes: Secondary builder in the pool. Handles research, market_analysis, spec, architecture, docs.

limitations: Less capable than cloud models for complex tasks
gemma4_local.notes: Support agent in the pool. Used for lightweight tasks and local processing.

kashclaw.notes: Specialized agent for trade_execution tasks. Not in the general builder pool.

limitations: Only handles market_analysis and prediction_signal tasks
bullbear_predictor.notes: Specialized agent for market_analysis and prediction_signal tasks.

quantumarb.notes: Specialized agent for arbitrage task type.

limitations: File-based only — cannot receive HTTP intents directly
bullbear_predictor.notes: Adversarial bull/bear signal generator forked from MiroFish. Outputs signals to ~/bullbear/signals/output/

limitations: Not a task executor — routes only
File-based only — polls inbox for intents
kashclaw.notes: Handles spot, margin, arb, scalping, hedging, algorithmic organ types

limitations: Strategy generation quality depends on signal inputs
kloutbot.notes: Upgraded to handle submit_goal, check_status, replan. Port 8765.

perplexity_research.notes: Secondary builder for specs, docs, architecture. Port 8766.

claude_cowork.notes: Best for implementation, refactoring, test writing. Port 8767. Primary builder in pool.

system_lessons.title: Mesh friction is learnable operational signal, not just transport noise
system_lessons.summary: Dropped messages, TTL expiry, and no-subscriber events show where the system's intended energy transfer is being lost. The mesh should treat those failures as training data for routing and subscription hygiene.

system_lessons.title: Agent churn should be scored as system instability
system_lessons.summary: Repeated register-deregister cycles are not harmless bookkeeping. They are evidence of unstable orchestration or supervision and should feed trust and routing decisions.

system_lessons.title: Revenue learning must join execution outcomes with orchestration intent
system_lessons.summary: To evolve recursively, the system has to connect why a plan was created, what signals were emitted, and what economic outcome followed.

system_lessons.title: Security and validation events belong in the learning loop
system_lessons.summary: Operational safety is part of system intelligence. Validation errors and security events should shape prompts, routing, and execution gates.

system_policy_candidates.title: Daily mesh hygiene reflection
system_policy_candidates.rationale: Summarize dropped-message causes and promote targeted routing/subscription fixes instead of leaving delivery friction buried in logs.

system_policy_candidates.title: Churn-aware routing and supervision review
system_policy_candidates.rationale: Agents with elevated churn should be deprioritized or supervised more tightly until stability recovers.

system_policy_candidates.title: Plan-to-execution lineage capture
system_policy_candidates.rationale: Attach plan IDs and intent lineage to downstream trades so successful and failed strategies can be ranked by actual economic output.
